name: last-validated-now-auto-stamped
tags: last_validated, memory staleness, memory audit, oldest memories, query pattern, memory__add, memory__add_batch, task:514edb14
body:
last_validated is now auto-stamped to datetime('now') on every memory__add/memory__add_batch call (both insert and update), as of task:514edb14. Before this, it existed in the schema (via the _NEW_COLUMNS migration) but was populated on 0/124 claude-hooks memories.

Why: there was no way to answer "when was this memory last confirmed accurate" without manually tracing files/content against git log for every staleness pass.

How to apply: to find staleness-audit priority candidates, query `sqlite3 ~/.claude/MEMORY.sqlite "SELECT name, last_validated FROM memories WHERE domain='<domain>' ORDER BY last_validated ASC NULLS FIRST"` — NULL entries are memories never touched since this fix landed (highest priority, unknown freshness), then oldest-timestamp entries next. Note last_validated bumps on ANY add/add_batch call, even one that only touches tags or fixes a typo — it is not proof the body's factual claims were re-verified against code, only that the row was written. Manual sqlite3 UPDATE (per CLAUDE.md's exposed-columns convention) is still the way to stamp last_validated during a /task-grooming or /task-introspection pass that confirms a memory accurate without editing it.
